paper_title: Sediment Core Analysis and Environmental Reconstruction at Maddox Lake

methods.description: Collection of sediment cores from Maddox Lake to study lake level changes and sedimentology.

steps.description: A single multidrive sediment core was collected using a Russian coring system 15 m from the modern edge of Maddox Lake.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment core using Russian coring system

steps.description: The core was opened on site, photographed, described, and wrapped in plastic wrap and aluminum foil for transport.
steps.searchable_summary: process and prepare sediment core for transport

methods.description: Age control using radiocarbon dating of organic materials to establish a timeline for sediment deposition.

steps.description: Radiocarbon dating (AMS 14C dating) of 17 discrete organic materials was performed.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS 14C dating on organic samples

steps.description: Samples were treated with an acid-base-acid protocol prior to combustion.

methods.description: Measurement of magnetic susceptibility and composition to analyze sediment properties.

steps.description: Magnetic susceptibility was measured at 1-cm intervals using a Bartington MS2 meter.
steps.searchable_summary: measure magnetic susceptibility with Bartington MS2

steps.description: Samples were combusted at 550°C and 950°C to calculate %TOM and %TC.

methods.description: Use of XRF to determine elemental concentrations in sediment samples.

steps.description: XRF was measured at 1-cm intervals using a portable Olympus Vanta C-series pXRF.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRF analysis with Olympus Vanta C-series

methods.description: Measurement of grain size distribution in sediment samples to understand sediment characteristics.

steps.description: Grain size was measured using a Malvern Mastersizer 2000 after pretreatment.
steps.searchable_summary: measure grain size with Malvern Mastersizer 2000

methods.description: PCA to explore relationships among various sediment properties and determine significant patterns.

steps.description: PCA was conducted based on normalized data and Euclidean distance.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA on normalized sediment data

steps.description: A PCA scatterplot was generated using the first and second principal component eigenvectors.
